name: kbd
title: Kbd
description: A single keyboard key rendered as a native <kbd> key cap, plus KbdGroup to compose a full shortcut. Semantic tokens (mod, cmd, ctrl, alt, shift, enter, esc, tab, space, backspace, delete, arrows) render the correct glyph for the visitor's platform (⌘ on macOS, Ctrl elsewhere) with a matching spoken label, since glyphs like ⌘/⌥ are unreliably announced by screen readers. Non-interactive: no tab stop, no ARIA widget role. KbdGroup wraps its keys in role=group with an auto-computed aria-label so a shortcut like Cmd+K announces as one clean phrase instead of each key and the separator being read separately.
useWhen: Documenting a keyboard shortcut next to the control it triggers (a Button's label, a Tooltip, a menu item)
Showing a cross-platform shortcut that should read ⌘ on macOS and Ctrl on Windows/Linux (use token="mod")
Composing a multi-key combination (e.g. Cmd+K) with KbdGroup so it announces as a single phrase
avoidWhen: The key itself needs to be interactive or trigger an action (use a Button)
Describing a mouse or pointer gesture rather than a keyboard key
Long-form instructions better suited to prose (e.g. "press and hold")

keyboard: Non-interactive: Kbd and KbdGroup add no tab stop and receive no keyboard focus
Not a form control or button — activating the described shortcut is the surrounding UI's responsibility, not this component's
accessibility.targetSize: not applicable — decorative/informational, not a pointer target
notes: Platform detection is SSR-safe: the initial render always reports the non-Mac glyph set so server and first client paint match exactly; a useEffect corrects to the Mac glyph set after mount with no hydration mismatch.

Glyphs (⌘, ⌥, ⇧, ⏎, ⎋, ␣, ⌫, ⌦, arrows) are aria-hidden and paired with a visually-hidden spoken label (e.g. "Command", "Option", "Escape") because symbols are inconsistently announced across screen readers.
KbdGroup renders role=group with an aria-label auto-computed from its parts' spoken labels (e.g. "Command K"); the decorative separator between keys is always aria-hidden so it is never read aloud.

When KbdGroup cannot auto-compute a label (a composed Kbd child has non-string children) and no explicit aria-label is passed, a dev-only console warning flags the missing accessible name — mirroring ToggleGroup's required-name guard.
The key cap keeps a visible border in every state (forced-colors:border-[CanvasText]) so it reads as a physical key even when Windows High Contrast overrides background/shadow colors.
